title: Match Types
seoTitle: Game of Darts Match Types | BarkinMad Studios
description: Understand the main darts formats in Game of Darts, including X01, Cricket, Snooker, Bob's 27, 1 to 20, and party modes.
summary: Game of Darts covers scoring, practice, and party formats rather than the arcade loops used by the ZX Series.
heroImageAlt: Game of Darts match types artwork

sections.heading: Match Types
paragraphs: Game of Darts supports a wide range of match formats, allowing you to play anything from a quick practice game to a full tournament-style contest. Match settings can be customised before every game to suit your preferred style of play.
Whether you're practising on your own, competing with friends or preparing for league matches, there's a match format to suit every occasion.

sections.heading: Legs and Sets
paragraphs: Traditional Match games are played over one or more legs. Multiple legs can be grouped into sets, allowing you to recreate the formats used in professional darts tournaments.
Winning the required number of legs wins the set, while winning the required number of sets wins the overall match.

sections.heading: Customising Your Match
paragraphs: Before starting a game you can customise the match to suit your preferences. Available options depend on the selected game mode.
Settings are designed to let both casual and competitive players create the experience they want.
bullets: Choose the starting score (301, 501 or 701 where supported).

sections.heading: Choosing the Right Match
paragraphs: Quick single-leg games are ideal for practising checkouts and improving consistency. Longer multi-leg and multi-set matches provide a more competitive experience and allow statistics to build over the course of the match.
Experiment with different formats to find the style that best matches your level of play.

images.alt: Artwork for Game of Darts match types
images.caption: Match type artwork

featureShowcases.heading: Choose the right format before play
featureShowcases.summary: Match Types explains how players move from a casual game into the ruleset that fits the session, from X01 to Cricket, Snooker, Bob's 27, and party formats.
body: This matters because the setup decision shapes the whole match: scoring rules, player count, legs, sets, and the kind of practice players get from the session.
Players use this page when deciding whether they want a quick leg, a tournament-style match, a practice game, or a ruleset that suits a group night.
bullets: Explains practical differences between supported formats.
Helps players choose between short sessions and longer match structures.
Connects format choice to setup, scoring, and strategy.

image.alt: Game of Darts match types artwork
image.caption: Match type artwork is used as the current visual placeholder for format selection.

featureShowcases.heading: Configure the session around the players
featureShowcases.summary: Once a format is chosen, players can tune settings such as starting score, legs, sets, player count, and DartBot participation where supported.
body: This matters because a quick practice leg and a competitive evening need different setup choices. Game of Darts keeps those decisions near the start of the match workflow.
Players use setup options to match the app to the board, the number of players, and the amount of time they have.
bullets: Supports quick games and tournament-style setups.
Keeps DartBot participation part of the match workflow.
Lets players experiment with different practice and competition formats.

image.alt: Game of Darts main setup screen for choosing a match format
image.caption: The main setup screenshot is reused as the current placeholder for configurable match setup.
